title: tapirira guianensis
intro: Tapirira guianensis is a plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae, commonly known as the poison tree due to its toxic compounds. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tapirira guianensis include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and insect repellent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, bark, and roots are often used to create infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that are used to treat various ailments.
